name: python-can (CAN Bus Interface for Python)

auth.notes: Hardware CAN interface; OS permissions required

performance.notes: Hardware-bound; Python overhead ~1ms; not for hard real-time bus timing

agent_readiness.notes: CAN bus library for Python — automotive/industrial. import can. bus = can.interface.Bus(bustype='socketcan', channel='can0', bitrate=500000). msg = can.Message(arbitration_id=0x123, data=[0x01, 0x02, 0x03]). bus.send(msg). for msg in bus: print(msg). Async: async with can.AsyncBufferedReader() as reader. Listeners: Printer, Logger, Notifier. Log formats: .asc, .blf, .csv, .db. Interfaces: SocketCAN, PEAK, Kvaser, Vector, SLCAN, virtual (testing). cantools for DBC file parsing. Used in automotive ECU testing, robotics, industrial automation.

gotchas: Hardware CAN interface required (or virtual for testing)
SocketCAN requires Linux and ip link setup
LGPL-3.0 license — check compatibility for commercial products
cantools (DBC parsing) is a separate package
best_when: Automotive ECU testing and CAN bus monitoring
Logging and replaying CAN bus traffic
CAN bus protocol development and debugging
avoid_when: Hard real-time CAN timing requirements (use C/embedded)
summary: python-can provides CAN bus communication for Python. can.interface.Bus(bustype='socketcan', channel='can0', bitrate=500000). bus.send(Message(arb_id=0x123, data=[0x01])). for msg in bus: iterates received messages. Async support. Log/replay .asc/.blf files. Interfaces: SocketCAN, PEAK, Kvaser, Vector, virtual. cantools for DBC signal decoding. Used in automotive ECU testing, industrial automation. LGPL-3.0. Linux SocketCAN preferred interface.
